programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic Premier League match. Here, a look back to 1998 and a four-goal thriller between Spurs and Man Utd at White Hart Lane.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: Beaten on Boxing Day, Newcastle stay on the road for a trip to Scott Parker's Burnley, who remain in the bottom three - six points adrift from Premier League safety. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Bournemouth Hlts
programme.desc: A Bournemouth side in freefall arrive at Stamford Bridge for a Premier League contest with an up-and-down Chelsea, who have only posted one win in their last five. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Forest and Everton meet for the second time this December. At the start of the month, Everton were resounding winners, putting three unanswered goals past Sean Dyche's Forest. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: The pressure is on for West Ham in this pivotal Premier League clash with Brighton to close out the year. The Hammers are 18th, now without a win in seven after defeat to Fulham. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: Arsenal v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: On a big night at the Emirates, league leaders Arsenal host an Aston Villa side on an 11-game winning run, the seventh of which coming against the Gunners earlier this month. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: Man Utd v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: Manchester United face Rob Edwards' winless Wolves in the Premier League. United are searching for back-to-back to wins at Old Trafford after beating Newcastle here on Boxing Day. (30.12)

programme.title: PL: Liverpool v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: The champions remain at Anfield after beating Wolves 2-1 here as Leeds come next for Liverpool. With ex-Evertonian Dominic Calvert-Lewin in the goals, Leeds are five games unbeaten. (01.01)

programme.title: PL: Crystal Palace v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: Three wins on the spin and Fulham are breathing down the necks of their London rivals Crystal Palace here, who tasted a third consecutive loss in the Premier League on Sunday. (01.01)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: Bouncing back from consecutive Premier League defeats, another stern test awaits Spurs as Thomas Frank returns to the Gtech Community Stadium to face former side Brentford. (01.01)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: Manchester City's title bid meets a Sunderland-shaped obstacle as Regis Le Bris' impressive Black Cats take on Pep Guardiola's serial Premier League winners at the Stadium of Light. (01.01)

programme.title: PL: Aston Villa v N. Forest Hlts
programme.desc: A chance for Villa to bounce back after a midweek drubbing at the Emirates as Unai Emery's side welcome 17th-placed Forest, who are aiming to avoid a fourth consecutive defeat. (03.01)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: Brighton play host to Burnley at the Amex Stadium in the Premier League. (03.01)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Wolves play host to West Ham at Molineux in the Premier League. (03.01)

programme.title: SNF: Bournemouth v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: On Saturday Night Football, league leaders Arsenal - who ran riot in a 4-1 demolition midweek - begin 2026 at the Vitality, facing a Bournemouth side on a 10-game winless run. (03.01)

programme.title: PL: Spurs v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: It's Sunderland at home for Thomas Frank's Spurs after their first goalless draw in 137 Premier League games. The Black Cats held Manchester City to a draw in the week. (04.01)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Coming away 2-0 winners from the City Ground, Everton are back at the Hill Dickinson Stadium for a Premier League encounter with Keith Andrews' in-form Brentford. (04.01)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: Unbeaten in four, Marco Silva's impressive Fulham provide another test for the champions Liverpool, who come from a goalless draw - the first of Arne Slot's Anfield tenure. (04.01)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle v C. Palace Hlts
programme.desc: Stemming the flow of three consecutive losses at home to Fulham, Palace go to Tyneside next to play Newcastle. The Magpies ended 2025 with a 3-1 win on the road to Burnley. (04.01)

programme.title: PL: Man City v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: Falling behind leaders Arsenal, what can Manchester City do in reply on Super Sunday against a Chelsea side coming to grips with the midweek dismissal of manager Enzo Maresca? (04.01)

programme.title: PL Stories: Pochettino's Spurs
programme.desc: Ahead of the World Cup, revisit the rise of current USA manager Mauricio Pochettino's Tottenham; the team that transformed Spurs from top-four hopefuls into genuine challengers.

programme.title: Transfer 360: John Obi Mikel
programme.desc: John Obi Mikel signed a contract with Manchester United, but ended up moving to Chelsea. Get the inside story of one of the Premier League's most controversial transfer deals.

programme.title: Transfer 360: Gareth Barry
programme.desc: Find out the inside story on Premier League record-appearance-holder Gareth Barry's failed move from Aston Villa to Liverpool and how Manchester City swooped in instead.

programme.desc: Find out the inside story on Premier League record-appearance-holder Gareth Barry's failed move from Aston Villa to Liverpool and how Manchester City swooped in instead.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccerbox
programme.desc: Gary Neville reunites with former Manchester United colleague Louis Saha to relive some of the greatest games and goals of one of the Premier League's most lethal marksmen.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccerbox
programme.desc: Gary Neville is joined by former French international defender William Gallas to look back on his greatest games playing for Chelsea, Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur.

programme.title: PL Years 03/04: Invincibles
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic season in the Premier League, as Arsene Wenger's Arsenal steamrollered all opposition on their way to the title as they completed a remarkable unbeaten season.

programme.title: PL Retro: Newcastle v Man Utd 1996
programme.desc: Relive a classic game from the 1996-97 Premier League season as title chasers Newcastle and Manchester United meet at St James' Park. It would be a memorable afternoon for the Magpies.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 15
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in November 2015. City needed a win to go back top of the table, while Liverpool were adapting to life under Jurgen Klopp.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 13
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in 2013. City needed three points to close the gap at the top to Man Utd, while the Reds were chasing a top four spot.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/L'Pool '95
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Man United host Liverpool in October 1995, as Eric Cantona returned from his lengthy suspension in a thrilling 2-2 draw.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:L'Pool/Man U '97
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Liverpool meet Manchester United at Anfield in 1997, in a match that had a happy outcome for the Red Devils.

programme.title: Best Premier League Goals 21/22
programme.desc: A compilation of breathtaking goals during the 2021-2022 Premier League season, including long shots, solo goals and bicycle kicks from some of the top flight's finest footballers.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Manchester United
programme.desc: Enjoy the best Premier League goals scored by Manchester United over the years. See strikes from legends of the game including Ryan Giggs, David Beckham and more.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Manchester City
programme.desc: A look at the very best goals scored by Manchester City in the history of the Premier League.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:L'Pool/Man U '01
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Liverpool meet Manchester United in 2001, as a Michael Owen brace guided the home side to victory.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/L'Pool '10
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Manchester United meet Liverpool in 2010, with a Dimitar Berbatov hat-trick proving the difference between the two teams.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/L'Pool '14
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Man United host Liverpool in March 2014 in a remarkable contest that saw the visitors awarded three penalties.

programme.title: PL Greatest : Chelsea/Man Utd 2012
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic Premier League match. Here, rewind to 2012 for a memorable, ill-tempered clash between high-flying Chelsea and Man Utd at Stamford Bridge.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: As those above them continue ticking over, what can Burnley do on the South Coast to improve their chances of Premier League survival? Scott Parker takes his side to Bournemouth. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Sunderland follow a derby day to remember with a visit to Brighton. The hosts, three points adrift of Regis Le Bris' side, lost on the road against Liverpool last weekend. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Man City v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Hitting their stride at a pivotal time in the season, Manchester City are back at the Etihad to take on West Ham. City can temporarily leapfrog leaders Arsenal with a win. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Wolves, desperately unlucky in the capital last week, face Brentford in the Premier League. Jordan Henderson netted for the Bees in his side's one-all draw with Leeds previously. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Spurs v Liverpool
programme.desc: A big test awaits for Thomas Frank and Spurs after a limp display against Forest as the champions Liverpool, looking to go six unbeaten, visit the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Everton v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: Lining up at the Hill Dickinson Stadium for the last time this year, Everton host Arsenal on Saturday Night Football. The Gunners needed two own goals to beat Wolves last weekend. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Leeds v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: On Saturday Night Football, Leeds attempt to extend an unbeaten Premier League run stretching three games with high-flying Crystal Palace the visitors at Elland Road. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Aston Villa v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Villa target a 10th consecutive win in all competitions on Super Sunday as the focus turns to Manchester United, who come from an exhilarating eight-goal thriller earlier this week. (21.12)

programme.title: MNF: Fulham v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: Lifting themselves clear of the drop zone with a 3-0 win over Spurs, it's Fulham at Craven Cottage next on Monday Night Football for Sean Dyche's ever-improving Nottingham Forest. (22.12)

programme.title: FNF: Man Utd v Newcastle
programme.desc: Friday Night Football comes from Old Trafford as Manchester United take on Newcastle in the Premier League's sole Boxing Day fixture. This time last year, the Magpies won here 2-0. (26.12)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Fulham
programme.desc: Searching for a much-needed win, West Ham host in-form London rivals Fulham. The Hammers sit in the drop zone with just 11 points to their name following a 3-0 defeat at City. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Bournemouth
programme.desc: Coming from a 2-0 win at Molineux, with two goals from Keane Lewis-Potter, Brentford take on Bournemouth, whose winless run stretched to eight matches previously. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Everton
programme.desc: Having ended a seven-game losing run to claim a precious point on the South Coast, can Burnley add three this time with the Premier League visit of Everton to Turf Moor? (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Arsenal v Brighton
programme.desc: Spending Christmas at the summit, title hopefuls Arsenal take on a Brighton side winless in their last four matches. Viktor Gyokores netted for the Gunners in a win over Everton. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Liverpool v Wolves
programme.desc: Bringing three points home from the capital, but at the cost of injury to their record signing, the champions Liverpool welcome Wolves to Anfield in the Premier League. (27.12)

programme.title: SNF: Chelsea v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: It's a Christmas cracker at Stamford Bridge as the Premier League's form team Aston Villa - seven points clear of Enzo Maresca's Chelsea - target an incredible 11th straight win. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: Four unbeaten in the top flight, including a 4-1 demolition of Palace, Leeds go to Sunderland on Super Sunday. The Black Cats earned a point on the road against Brighton last week. (28.12)

programme.title: PL: Crystal Palace v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: For the final time this year, Crystal Palace and Tottenham Hotspur take to the pitch for Premier League action as the two London rivals meet at Selhurst Park on Super Sunday. (28.12)

programme.desc: Tommy Paul's 10th tour-level final pits the American against Peru's Ignacio Buse, who enters a maiden tour-level title match. Paul was a clay-court champion in Houston in April. (23.05)

programme.title: ATP Final: Miami
programme.desc: Jannik Sinner's chance to join an exclusive list of Sunshine Double winners arrives as the Italian world number two faces the explosive Czech Jiri Lehecka in the Miami Open final. (29.03)

programme.title: WTA Final: Miami
programme.desc: Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff share the stage in a final for the fourth time, with hometown favourite Gauff bidding to deny defending Miami champion Sabalenka the Sunshine Double. (28.03)

programme.title: Tennis Highlights
programme.desc: In a bid to reach the Stuttgart quarter-finals, the United States' Frances Tiafoe - the sixth seed here - looks to overcome Australia's Rinky Hijikata in the round of 16. (11.06)

programme.title: Tennis Highlights
programme.desc: Canada's Felix Auger-Aliassime gets his grass-court season underway in 's-Hertogenbosch as the top seed encounters Márton Fucsovics in the second round. (11.06)

programme.title: ATP Final: Toronto
programme.desc: Ben Shelton features in his first ATP Masters final as the 22-year-old eyes world number six status against Karen Khachanov, who ends a seven-year wait to compete on this stage. (07.08)

programme.title: WTA Final: Montreal
programme.desc: Rising Canadian star Victoria Mboko's miracle Montreal run continues as the 18-year-old meets four-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka in her first tour-level final. (07.08)

programme.title: ATP Final: Geneva
programme.desc: In what was a chance for Novak Djokovic to join Jimmy Connors and Roger Federer in winning a 100th tour-level title, the 38-year-old Serb battled Hubert Hurkacz in the Geneva Open final.

programme.title: WTA Final: Strasbourg
programme.desc: With a first career clay-court title on the line, a resurgent Emma Navarro meets the teenage world number nine Victoria Mboko in the final of the Internationaux de Strasbourg. (23.05)

programme.title: ATP Final: Monte Carlo
programme.desc: In a winner-takes-all showdown for world number one status, rivals Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner meet for the first time this year to determine the Monte-Carlo Masters champion. (12.04)

programme.title: ATP Final: Doha
